BTC-USD.key_risk: April 15 exchange inflow spike (11,000 BTC/hr at $75K resistance) + BTC dominance already at 58% limits further rotation tailwind; daily RSI 73 approaching overbought
BTC-USD.head_trader_note: Near-unanimous bullish consensus (8/10) anchored by institutional conviction: $921M ETF inflows, 270K BTC whale accumulation (largest since 2013), exchange reserves at 9-year lows, and Strategic Bitcoin Reserve catalyst due July 2026. Only mean-reversion (bearish, Z-score +1.89) and breakout (neutral, failed $77.5K confirmation) dissent — both valid concerns but outweighed by structural tailwinds. Enter at current support cluster $74,200-$74,600 targeting $77,127 initial / $79,500 extension; stop below 38.2% Fib at $71,800.

ETH-USD.key_risk: $292M Kelp DeFi exploit + $5.4B Aave rsETH outflows create DeFi confidence overhang; BTC dominance at 57.5% historically suppresses altcoin performance; mean-reversion signal warns 10.4% above 50-SMA
ETH-USD.head_trader_note: Moderate bullish consensus (5/10) with a meaningful neutral bloc — the split reflects genuine uncertainty between strong ETF inflow catalysts and DeFi security headwinds. Glamsterdam hard fork (78% fee reduction, H1 2026), record $11.82B ETF AUM, and hourly RSI 25.7 (deeply oversold) support a bounce trade, but this is lower conviction than BTC. Size smaller; wait for hourly EMA reclaim above 2,300 as additional entry confirmation. Stop below Kelp-overhang support at $2,170.

SOL-USD.key_risk: $270M Drift exploit security overhang; spot buying demand collapsed 80% since March 22; daily ADX 19.5 (trendless); price below 50-EMA with 200-SMA at $127 (50% above current)
SOL-USD.head_trader_note: No trade. The technical picture is disqualifying: ADX 19.5 (below threshold for any strategy), EMAs not aligned, and price below the 50-EMA in a structurally weak environment. Two outright bearish calls (momentum_ranker, sector_rotation) with zero bullish votes — this consensus is decisive. Firedancer/Alpenglow catalysts are H2 2026, too distant. Wait for clean reclaim above $89 resistance on above-average volume before re-evaluating.

BNB-USD.key_risk: DOJ probe overhang caps institutional demand; 48% decline in CEX volumes is a structural headwind for BNB utility; hard fork catalyst could disappoint if broader crypto risk-off continues post-FOMC (April 28-29 overlap)
BNB-USD.head_trader_note: Weak consensus — 8/10 neutral, only breakout and catalyst traders bullish — but the April 28 Osaka/Mendel hard fork provides a tight, time-bound binary catalyst that warrants a small speculative position. This is purely an event trade, not a trend trade: hourly Bollinger compression (0.0166 width) signals coiling energy, and the BNB burn ($1.32B on April 15) removed supply. If entering, keep size minimal (half-unit), place stop tight at $610, and exit by April 28 regardless of outcome.

XRP-USD.key_risk: Binary ETF risk — 7 Q2 2026 approvals pending; any rejection triggers sharp reversal. Large Coinbase XRP transfer ($119M on April 13) ambiguous but warrants monitoring. CLARITY Act could miss May window.
XRP-USD.head_trader_note: Strongest consensus in the basket (7/10 bullish) driven by the most compelling regulatory catalyst stack: SEC/CFTC commodity reclassification achieved, 7 spot ETF decisions due Q2 2026, CLARITY Act Senate floor vote expected May, Rakuten 44M-user integration live, whale accumulation at 10-month highs. Mean-reversion (bearish) correctly notes CCI 189 and Bollinger 0.897 overextension — respect this with a disciplined stop. The hourly RSI 29.4 pullback to $1.41 offers the entry; position sizing should reflect the binary ETF event risk. This is the highest-priority long in the portfolio.
